variants.rationale: Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.

variants.author: proposer
variants.rationale: Switch to a more robust ASR path and a SOAP-style stronger note model to recover missed clinical details and produce cleaner chart structure on choppy audio.

diff.description: Whisper plus SOAP note generation with balanced cleanup for fragmented recordings.

variants.author: proposer
variants.rationale: Keep the current ASR family but reduce over-aggressive preprocessing and ask for a more detailed note to improve completeness without changing too many components at once.

diff.description: Parakeet with gentler preprocessing and detailed note prompting for better completeness.

variants.author: proposer
variants.rationale: Test whether better transcription alone carries most of the gain by changing ASR and preprocessing while keeping generation relatively lightweight and brief.

diff.description: Whisper-first lightweight note variant emphasizing transcript recovery over prompt expansion.

variants.author: proposer
variants.rationale: Swap to parakeet and tighten preprocessing to reduce stray multilingual fragments, transcript leakage, and awkward note wording caused by noisy spans.

variants.author: proposer
variants.rationale: Keep the stronger Whisper baseline but shift generation toward fuller chart-ready capture by preserving more quiet context and using a more complete note style.

variants.author: proposer
variants.rationale: Test parakeet with recall-friendly cleanup and a detailed note style to improve allergy, vitals, and return-precaution capture without enhancement artifacts.

variants.author: proposer
variants.rationale: Switching ASR to parakeet while tightening cleanup and using a SOAP-oriented prompt directly targets transcript leakage, fragmented multilingual artifacts, and weak section completeness.

variants.author: proposer
variants.rationale: Keeping whisper but shifting the generator toward a stricter SOAP structure with stronger preprocessing should test whether the main issue is formatting discipline and cleanup rather than the ASR backbone.

variants.author: proposer
variants.rationale: This variant isolates ASR impact with a lighter prompt change, aiming to preserve rich detail while reducing omissions from fragmented recognition through parakeet plus slightly stricter turn detection.

variants.author: proposer
variants.rationale: Swap to the alternate ASR path and ease preprocessing slightly to reduce transcript-artifact leakage while preserving low-volume clinical details such as meds, allergies, and return precautions.

variants.author: proposer
variants.rationale: Keep the stronger generator model but move from SOAP to a more detailed note style so the note is less templated and more likely to retain full negatives, vitals, allergy context, and explicit precautions.

variants.author: proposer
variants.rationale: Test whether tighter speech segmentation with the same high-capability note model improves factual extraction and removes stray foreign-language fragments without changing the overall SOAP framing.

variants.author: proposer
variants.rationale: Switch to whisper for potentially cleaner medical transcription and pair it with a more complete note prompt to improve factual capture, return precautions, and removal of transcript leakage.

diff.description: Whisper transcription with a detailed clinical note prompt to improve completeness and reduce transcript leakage.

variants.author: proposer
variants.rationale: Keep the stronger parakeet baseline but make preprocessing more assertive so fragmented or stray language tokens are cleaned up before note generation, while using a detailed prompt to preserve specific follow-up guidance.

diff.description: Parakeet with stronger cleanup and a detailed note prompt for cleaner, more complete follow-up notes.

2026-04-18T13-25-31__2026-0.rationale: Switch to a more robust ASR path and a SOAP-style stronger note model to recover missed clinical details and produce cleaner chart structure on choppy audio.
2026-04-18T13-25-31__2026-0.judgeJustification: The artifact preserves many core history and exam facts and maintains a basic clinical-note structure, but it falls short of the expected standard. Its main problems are missing key clinical details such as allergy and full vitals, a generic and incomplete plan without explicit return precautions, and obvious meta/evaluation leakage that makes the note feel less professional and less chart-ready.

changes.before: Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.
changes.after: Whisper plus SOAP note generation with balanced cleanup for fragmented recordings.
changes.summary: description changed from `Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.` to `Whisper plus SOAP note generation with balanced cleanup for fragmented recordings.`
params -> pipeline config -> asr -> model changed from `parakeet` to `whisper`
params -> pipeline config -> note generation -> model changed from `gpt-5-mini` to `gpt-5`
params -> pipeline config -> note generation -> prompt style changed from `brief` to `soap`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> denoise changed from `off` to `mild`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> silence trimming changed from `aggressive` to `moderate`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> vad changed from `strict` to `balanced`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> voice enhancement changed from `off` to `on`

2026-04-18T13-25-31__2026-0.rationale: Test whether better transcription alone carries most of the gain by changing ASR and preprocessing while keeping generation relatively lightweight and brief.
2026-04-18T13-25-31__2026-0.judgeJustification: The artifact captures the high-level impression of a viral upper respiratory infection and uses a recognizable SOAP structure, but it underperforms on core clinical fidelity and chart readiness. Major omissions include the exact symptom timeline, worse-at-night history, dextromethorphan, penicillin allergy, most vitals, key lung and throat exam findings, and the explicit return precautions and follow-up window. The note is further weakened by transcript/leakage artifacts and meta commentary, making it less complete, less professional, and less actionable than the expected note.

Its main weaknesses are substantial factual omissions, a weak and nonspecific plan with missing return precautions, and a nonclinical presentation style that includes transcript leakage and editorial commentary instead of polished documentation.

2026-04-18T13-27-45__2026-0.judgeJustification: This artifact has a recognizable SOAP structure and preserves the broad diagnosis and some major symptoms, but it falls well short of the expected note on factual completeness and clinical polish. The biggest problems are omission of key vitals, exam findings, allergy and medication details, and especially the weak follow-up and missing explicit return precautions. Non-clinical transcript leakage and meta commentary further reduce confidence and chart-readiness.

2026-04-18T13-30-04__2026-0.rationale: Keeping whisper but shifting the generator toward a stricter SOAP structure with stronger preprocessing should test whether the main issue is formatting discipline and cleanup rather than the ASR backbone.
2026-04-18T13-30-04__2026-0.judgeJustification: The artifact preserves the main clinical picture and core assessment, but it falls short of the expected note in several clinically important areas. Key omissions include the penicillin allergy, several vitals, and the explicit return precautions; the plan is also noticeably more generic. In addition, awkward phrasing and meta-commentary about transcript drift and comparison to a golden note make the document less professional and less chart-ready.

2026-04-18T13-30-04__2026-0.judgeJustification: The artifact captures the broad clinical picture of a likely viral upper respiratory infection, but it falls short of the expected note because it omits multiple important history, vital, exam, and plan details, and it fails to document explicit return precautions. The SOAP structure helps readability, but unsupported transcript fragments and meta commentary make the note feel less professional and less chart-ready than the target.

2026-04-18T13-34-21__2026-0.judgeJustification: This artifact preserves the broad clinical picture and basic structure, but it falls short of the expected note on both factual completeness and clinical polish. The biggest problems are omission of key data such as allergy and full vitals, a weak and nonspecific plan that drops explicit return precautions, and hallucinated/meta content that breaks the clinical-note frame. Overall, it is partially usable but clearly below the target standard.
